title: Sanitizers, Static Analysis, and Fuzzing · Native Transfer Build
summary: An independent maintainer ports the layered memory-defect test matrix to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of layered memory-defect test matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer and fully instrumented MemorySanitizer environments for their distinct defect classes.
Run Clang and GCC static analyzers, preserve paths and assumptions, classify findings, and regression-test repairs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Asan Lsan Evidence
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 2005. Produce bounded C evidence to classify a defect against complementary native tools; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.

For this lab demonstration of cmem-tool-limit-matrix, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-tool-limit-matrix-1rsgqc */" for changed case 4716.
